title: Agent Control Contract — Capabilities Manifest
description: The neutral, machine-readable capabilities manifest: the discovery spine of the Agent Control Contract (ACC). A conformant agent emits this object from the one-shot `--capabilities` entrypoint and (later) from the live `agent://capabilities` resource. agentctl consumes ONLY this contract, never an agent-specific shape. The `surfaces{}` block is the SINGLE discovery point for every control-plane surface: a key ABSENT means the surface is unbuilt and the consumer degrades gracefully; never branch on a value that is not advertised here. VERSIONING: `contract_version` is major.minor; additive growth (new fields/tools/metrics/surfaces keys) bumps MINOR and a consumer MUST tolerate it (this object and its open sub-objects use additionalProperties:true); a consumer refuses ONLY an unknown MAJOR. NEUTRAL TOKENS: the contract uses neutral tokens only — agent_version, agent://, agent_, AGENT_*. SECRETS: this manifest NEVER carries credentials — intelligence is structural (transport scheme + endpoint count) only.

contract_version.description: The Agent Control Contract version this binary speaks, major.minor (current value "2.0"). Negotiation is on MAJOR only: a consumer refuses an instance whose MAJOR it does not understand and tolerates any unknown additive content within a MAJOR it knows. In contract 1.0 the network is the substrate: a conformant agent serves its entire control surface over mTLS HTTPS; there are no non-HTTP transports and no local exec surface.

agent_version.description: Build/version string of the agent binary. Free-form, implementation-defined. REQUIRED at the root.

build_features.description: OPAQUE list of compile-time features present in this binary (example tokens include tls, serve-mcp, serve-https, a2a, cron, metrics, otel). The contract does NOT freeze the membership of this set and consumers MUST NOT branch on individual values — feature presence is advertised structurally via surfaces{}. Treat build_features as diagnostic metadata only; new tokens are additive and must be tolerated.

mode.description: The configured run shape. NOT a frozen enum (open, additive-tolerant): the defined set is once | loop | reactive | schedule, but a consumer MUST tolerate an unknown mode string.

model.description: Operator-declared model identifier, or null when unset. Metadata only, NEVER a secret.

mcp_servers.description: Operator-declared MCP client servers, structural name + trifecta tags only — never the endpoint URL or headers. Contract 1.0: MCP servers are remote HTTPS endpoints, so there is no argv to leak.

a2a_peers.description: Operator-declared A2A delegation peers, structural name + transport scheme only — never the endpoint URL.

exec_enabled.description: Whether the local exec/tool surface is enabled for this run.

allow_trifecta.description: Whether the lethal-trifecta combination is permitted.

identity.description: Downward-API identity block. DESCRIPTIVE only — never load-bearing for placement/scheduling. run_id is always present (a ULID is synthesized if unset); the Kubernetes-sourced fields are string-or-null. A conformant agent populates these from the downward-API env vars noted per field (AGENT_*); an empty env value is coerced to unset (null).

run_id.description: Always present. Stable run identity (ULID if not supplied). Populated from env AGENT_RUN_ID.

instance.description: Pod name. Downward API metadata.name via AGENT_POD_NAME. null when unset.

uid.description: Pod UID. Downward API metadata.uid via AGENT_POD_UID. null when unset.

node.description: Node name. Downward API spec.nodeName via AGENT_NODE_NAME. null when unset.

namespace.description: Namespace. Downward API metadata.namespace via AGENT_POD_NAMESPACE. null when unset.

intelligence.description: STRUCTURAL intelligence summary — transport scheme + configured endpoint count + reachability. NEVER an endpoint URL or token (no embedded credentials). The full per-endpoint health view lives at agent://intelligence; the manifest carries only the bounded count.

transport.description: The PRIMARY endpoint's transport scheme, or null when no endpoint is configured / scheme is unrecognized. Contract 1.0 scheme set: https — intelligence is reached over an HTTPS URL.

endpoints.description: Count of configured intelligence endpoints (0 when unset; a single --intelligence element is 1).

healthy.description: SUM TYPE: a boolean last-known reachability, OR the literal string "unknown" on the one-shot pre-connect probe (nothing has connected yet). Consumers MUST handle both the boolean and the "unknown" string; codegen needs a hand-written deserializer for this field.

intelligence_summary.description: Coarse capability hints for placement (no secrets). Open object; fields are advisory and may be omitted. Only `toolmode` is emitted by default; `max_context_hint` is omitted unless configured.

max_context_hint.description: Operator-declared max-context hint, or null. Omitted-when-absent.

mcp_server.description: An MCP client server entry: structural name + trifecta tags only.

a2a_peer.description: An A2A delegation peer entry: structural name + transport scheme only (no address).

transport.description: Peer transport scheme. Contract 1.0 set: https | unknown (unknown when the endpoint scheme is unrecognized). A2A peers are dialed over mTLS HTTPS.

limits.description: The bounding box: per-run limits + supervisor fork-bomb tree caps. All values are integers. Open object; consumers tolerate additional limit keys.

max_children.description: Max direct children per node (tree cap).

max_total_subagents.description: Max total subagents in the tree (tree cap).

deadline_ms.description: Wall-clock deadline in ms (a large sentinel when no deadline is set).

surfaces.description: The graceful-degradation contract and SINGLE discovery point. Each control-plane surface is reported HONESTLY as served-or-not for THIS build/config; agentctl drives ONLY what is declared here. A key ABSENT means the surface is unbuilt — degrade gracefully; do NOT treat absence as an error. additionalProperties:true is mandatory (additive-tolerance): NEVER apply deny-unknown / additionalProperties:false here — new surface keys are additive and bump MINOR. Several keys are SUM TYPES (bool|string, bool|object) and require hand-written deserializers in any codegen target: codegen cannot derive these.

management.description: SUM TYPE (bool|string): the served self-MCP management address when configured (contract 1.0: an mTLS HTTPS URL, e.g. "https://0.0.0.0:8443"), else false. A conformant agent emits either the address string or the boolean false; codegen needs a hand-written deserializer.

oneOf.description: Management address — contract 1.0 serves the self-MCP over mTLS HTTPS, e.g. "https://0.0.0.0:8443".

operator_tools.description: Operator admin methods advertised on the management surface, invokable only by a Management-origin peer. Contract 1.0 spells these as the a2a.* admin JSON-RPC methods, in FROZEN order: a2a.Drain, a2a.LameDuck, a2a.Pause, a2a.Resume, a2a.Cancel. These are operator extensions, distinct from the A2A-protocol methods in surfaces.a2a (which keep the bare PascalCase spelling). `attach` is NOT a tool — it maps to subagent.send and is excluded. There is NO `force` tool (force = a second SIGTERM). Empty array when the build cannot serve the management surface. Items are typed as open strings (not a closed enum) for additive-tolerance: a consumer MUST tolerate an unknown additive tool name and refuse only at a new MAJOR.

a2a.description: SUM TYPE (bool|object): the A2A external-agent surface. false when this build does not serve A2A; otherwise an object advertising the served method set. Contract 1.0: this key advertises the COMPILED A2A capability independent of whether a listener is bound, so a no-serve build still advertises it. The normative method names are the A2A protocol's canonical BARE PascalCase spelling served over HTTPS JSON-RPC (POST /mcp); the legacy a2a.-prefixed spelling is accepted for back-compat but the bare form is canonical. Requires a hand-written deserializer (bool|object).

version.description: A2A protocol version this surface advertises (e.g. "1.0").

streaming.description: Whether status-level streaming (SendStreamingMessage + SubscribeToTask) is served. Contract 1.0: streaming is an SSE text/event-stream of StreamResponse frames terminated by the terminal task STATE plus stream close — there is no non-spec `final` flag.

methods.description: The served method names. Contract 1.0 canonical set is the A2A protocol's BARE PascalCase spelling over HTTPS JSON-RPC: SendMessage, GetTask, CancelTask, ListTasks, SendStreamingMessage, SubscribeToTask. The legacy a2a.-prefixed spelling (a2a.SendMessage, …) is accepted for back-compat. Open string items for additive-tolerance.

address.description: OPTIONAL dedicated A2A listener address. Contract 1.0: A2A is served on the same mTLS HTTPS /mcp listener as management, so this key is omitted by default; it is present only if a build serves A2A on its own separate address.

metrics.description: SUM TYPE (bool|string): the Prometheus /metrics scrape address when configured (e.g. ":9090"), else false. Carries the ADDRESS only — the byte-identical Prom-text resource (agent://metrics) and the capacity schema (agent://capacity) are out of manifest scope. Requires a hand-written deserializer (bool|string).

metrics_schema.description: FROZEN metrics-schema version, major.minor (current value "1.0"). Opaque version string: it gates which metric registry the consumer reads.

events.description: Whether the agent://events stream is served.

report_schema.description: FROZEN run-outcome report schema version, major.minor (current value "1.0").

exit_codes.description: The exit-code contract version, a major.minor version string (current value "1.0").

intelligence.description: Whether the agent://intelligence endpoint-health resource is served.

hot_reload.description: Whether the SIGHUP-triggered reload of the reloadable config subset is served.

config_schema.description: Whether --config-schema is available. PROVISIONAL: a true value is contract-declared.

cluster.description: Whether horizontal-scaling sharding + the capacity resource are present.

shard.description: SUM TYPE (string|null): this instance's "K/N" shard identity, or null when unsharded (N==1) or no cluster.

standby.description: Whether this instance is a standby (--standby); agentctl routes a directed assignment only to instances reporting standby:true.

claim.description: SUM TYPE (bool|object): when present, the claim styles this instance speaks. IMPORTANT: this key is OMITTED-when-absent, never false — a build without the claim path leaves the key out entirely (agentctl places a claim route only on instances advertising it). The schema allows bool|object so that absence validates and a hypothetical false (if ever emitted) still validates, but the contract intent is OMITTED, not false.

styles.description: Claim styles spoken. Defined set: tool, resource.
